institution.name: Massachusetts General Hospital
aliases: Harvard Medical School — Visiting Students Program (covering MGH placement)
institution.healthSystem: Mass General Brigham (clinical) / Harvard Medical School (academic)

searchTermsTried: Massachusetts General Hospital visiting medical student MGH Harvard clerkship VSLO site:massgeneral.org
searchProcess.robotsOrAccessNotes: MGH visiting-clerkship URL redirects to massgeneralbrigham.org. All department pages confirm: 'To arrange a [specialty] rotation at Mass General, apply through Harvard Medical School's Exchange Clerkship Program.' MGH does not run an independent visiting-MS lane.

candidateFindings.shortQuote: In order to arrange a surgical rotation at Mass General, you must apply through Harvard Medical School's Exchange Clerkship Program for Visiting Medical Students.
candidateFindings.specialtyDecision: Multispecialty per HMS catalog (Surgery, Radiology, Urology, Neurology, Radiation Oncology, Vascular all confirmed offering MGH placements)

candidateFindings.shortQuote: The Surgical Residency Program at Massachusetts General Hospital accepts graduates of international medical schools into our program. We only sponsor J-1 visas for preliminary positions.
candidateFindings.specialtyDecision: Surgery residency only (NOT visiting medical student)

negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: MGH does NOT run an independent visiting-medical-student program. All visiting-MS access is gated by Harvard Medical School Exchange Clerkship Program (same path as BWH — see MA/brigham-and-womens-hospital.json packet). MGH-specific international-applications page is for the Surgery RESIDENCY only and is NOT a USCE pathway.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: The MGH-specific page text for MGH visiting-students hub was not fully retrieved (redirects to massgeneralbrigham.org); inheriting HMS policy is the safest classification.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) SCHOOL_LEVEL_SOURCE — HMS Exchange Clerkship Program covers MGH placement; (b) final-year MS only; (c) international students eligible via VSLO with Oct-Mar window restriction (inherited from HMS); (d) $115 application fee per experience (HMS); (e) 1-3 months duration; (f) MGH surgery RESIDENCY (ERAS, J-1 prelim only) is a separate non-USCE lane and must not be confused with the visiting-MS lane.
plainEnglishSummary: MGH does not run its own visiting medical student program. All visiting medical student access goes through Harvard Medical School's Exchange Clerkship Program — same path as Brigham and Women's. Final-year medical students apply via VSLO. International students can apply but only for October-March rotations. $115 application fee per experience. Separately, MGH's Surgery Residency Program accepts IMG graduates through ERAS with J-1 visa sponsorship for prelim positions — that's a residency lane, not USCE.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because MGH was correctly identified as HMS-gated rather than overclaimed as an independent USCE pathway; the Surgery Residency IMG language was correctly classified RESIDENCY_ONLY rather than misread as an open IMG observership.
